definition: Current bank CET1 ratios at 13-14% represent a genuine structural break vs historical credit-equity episodes (4-8% CET1). SVB/Signature/First Republic failures in 2023 were contained because the system was well-capitalized enough to absorb losses. Activation probability is materially lower than historical base rates imply.
evidence: SVB/Signature/First Republic failures contained in 2023 — direct empirical evidence
CRE concentration at regional banks (28-30%) means loading exists even if threshold is higher
Qualitative direction clear: more buffer = higher activation threshold
open_questions: Does 2x capital halve activation probability or merely delay it?
What is the quantitative adjustment to banking channel severity estimates?
At what CRE loss rate do current capital buffers become insufficient?
